_label_note: EU Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered public support-hotline bait, obfuscated VAT/tax/passport phone-shaped fragments, dotted-date and filing-reference phone bait, role/placeholder emails, template passport values, EU registry/ISIN/LEI/tax numeric fragments, log-id amount fragments, placeholder country-code phones, and negated MAE/contingent-MNPI language. Full EU member-state national-ID breadth and broader semantic GDPR special-category coverage remain ideal-tier gaps.
_label_tier_note: EU Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

must_detect.matched_text:
Confidential Board–Compliance Briefing — Nordelectric Renewables SE (EU issuer listed on EuroVista Markets Amsterdam) — 28 May 2026.
Subject: MAR, GDPR (incl. Art 9), CSRD/SFDR alignment and crypto/cyber adjacent events; circulation limited to Insider List “Project Lapis”.
Crypto MNPI: Treasury is negotiating a private subscription for EURX stablecoin liquidity with Auriga Vault OÜ, coupled with a pilot tokenised REC issuance; not public and likely price-sensitive.
Commercial terms (inside information until Article 17 MAR disclosure): draft term sheet contemplates €40m green convertibles, 4.25% coupon, 30% conversion premium, and a volume-linked fee rebate from Auriga; do not discuss externally.
Cyber incident (DORA/NIS2): on 26 May we detected lateral movement in our cloud IAM; no customer outage, but exfiltration of 2 HR files with medical leave codes (GDPR Art 9 data) cannot be ruled out; incident not yet notified.
Cross-border transfer: forensic review involves our processor Datashift Pte Ltd (Singapore) under SCCs with a Transfer Impact Assessment; freeze any additional exports until DPO sign-off.
ESG/CSRD: preliminary Scope 3 recalculation indicates +18% upstream emissions, which would adjust SFDR PAI 4 and may trip the sustainability-linked loan margin ratchet in Q3; not in the public sustainability statement.
Insider list: per Article 18 MAR, add Dr. Milo Šarić (CFO), Ms. Liora Benet (Head of Treasury), and Mr. Oskar Mertens (CISO) to permanent/temporary sections as applicable; maintain timestamps and personal contacts.
Information barriers: only the “clean team” (folder: Lapis_Room) may access draft press text, wallet custodian due diligence, and emissions restatement tables; all other staff are wall-crossed only after formal gate approval.
Blackout and communications: pre-close blackout runs 01–30 June; no analyst guidance, no selective disclosure in ESG marketing, and strictly no investor soundings on crypto treasury before publication of inside information.
Reminder on tipping: do not forward vendor security questionnaires or wallet architecture diagrams to persons outside the list; even subject-line references can constitute unlawful disclosure under MAR.
Educational/operations-only note: the “Crypto Wallet Operations Handbook” and the “Climate Data Catalogue” are training materials reused in onboarding and contain process vocabulary but no deal-stage or price-sensitive content.
Contact for queries and to report suspected personal data breaches or wall-crossing errors: Ms. Elina Voracek, Compliance Officer, elina.voracek@nordelectric-se.eu, +420 990 114 775.
Next steps: prepare MAR announcement drafts in the format expected by the national competent authority, validate SCCs with the DPO, and schedule board sign-off; calendar holds labelled “Lapis MAR draft” are restricted and should not appear on shared agendas.
